# Description
<!-- orient the reviewer to the goal of this project and this PR -->

# Scope of Review
<!-- if the scope is narrow or you'd prefer focus on one aspect (function vs syntax) -->

# Timeline of Review
<!-- remind the reviewer of when you'd like this review completed by -->

# Links
<!-- link any important documentation or deliverables that are relevant to the code -->

# Pull Request Author Checklist
I have done my due diligence in confirming and/ or communicating:

- [ ] the goal of this PR and its larger project
- [ ] the specific concerns or questions I have for the reviewer
- [ ] the timeline of when review is desired to be completed and reviewer's capacity
- [ ] the links above include all relevant content
- [ ] the reviewer has access to all linked content (e.g. Sharepoint files, Infogram)
- [ ] the quality of documentation of the project throughout (e.g. code comments, folder READMEs)
- [ ] the content follows our style guidelines (or the relevant affiliates' guidelines)

# Pull Request Reviewer Checklist
This is for the reviewer's reference but no need to actually write out answers.

I have shared comments and questions that address the following:

- [ ] Is the goal of this work clear from and addressed by the content?
- [ ] Are there any functional issues or bugs in the code?
- [ ] Are there any stylistic changes you'd suggest?
- [ ] Is the documentation of the project sufficient to orient a reviewer or a potential future code re-user?
- [ ] Did the code or linked content raise any questions you'd want to share?
- [ ] Are there any nice-to-have suggestions that could elevate the work or be a learning opportunity for the author?
- [ ] Have I marked comments by their significance (e.g. blocking/non-blocking, suggestion/nitpick)?
